reasoning:
The Chicago Fed's NFCIRISK index currently sits at -0.576 for both the weeks ending Oct 17 and Oct 24, 2025. This indicates deeply loose/calm financial conditions, near historic bounds, but the index has completely flattened out recently, with multiple weeks showing absolutely zero change (-0.577, -0.577, -0.576, -0.576). 

1. For 2025-11-02 (very short horizon): Due to the index's recent perfect flatline and the publication schedule (data for Oct 31 or simply Oct 24 depending on exactly when resolution samples), the probability of a strict positive increase is relatively low. I estimate 35%.
2. For 2025-11-25 (1 month): The index has more time to break its current flat momentum. Being historically very loose, mean reversion predicts it will eventually rise. As risk events or minor volatility occur, a rise to above -0.576 is a coin flip. I estimate 50%.
3. For 2026-01-24 (3 months): A 3-month horizon is plenty of time for mean reversion (the index historical average is 0) and for normal spikes in financial volatility, especially with general market expectations of higher volatility towards early 2026. The asymmetry of risk (spikes up happen fast) makes it more likely than not that conditions will be tighter than the current localized bottom. I estimate 60%.
